page: Amazon Web Services c8i.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores based on the x86_64 Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor, running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for matrix multiplication. The instance is equipped with 96.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing a 2.0 GB RAM-to-vCPU ratio. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, relying on network-attached storage and baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong performance across compute and memory operations. In Geekbench testing, the instance achieves top-tier results in single-core performance, image processing, and text processing. Multi-core performance, software compilation, and ray tracing fall within the top 25%. Memory bandwidth tests show strong read performance for both cached small blocks and uncached large blocks, though write speeds for large blocks remain in the average tier. Cryptographic and integer operations, measured via Passmark, consistently rank in the top 25%. For specialized workloads, the instance demonstrates top-tier static web serving throughput and strong LLM inference speeds for small and medium models, though text generation for large 70B models drops to average.

From a resource-density perspective, the c8i.12xlarge offers high compute-to-RAM efficiency for CPU-bound tasks, utilizing high-speed DDR5 memory to prevent bottlenecks. However, the lack of bundled local storage means users must factor in the latency of external block storage. The 2.0 GB of memory per core represents a tradeoff, making this instance less suitable for memory-intensive databases but highly efficient for compute-heavy workloads. It is best suited for batch processing, software compilation, web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 96.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz, yielding a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, but provides a 22 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core, image processing, and text processing performance, alongside strong multi-core and cryptographic capabilities. While memory read bandwidth is strong, write bandwidth and large-model LLM text generation perform at average levels. This instance is optimized for compute-heavy tasks such as software compilation, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference, though the lack of local storage requires external block storage solutions.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 48 vCPUs, 96 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier single-core and image processing performance.

tagline: A compute-optimized x86 platform featuring high-speed DDR5 memory and advanced matrix extensions for intensive CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C at 3.9 GHz
96.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory with 2.0 GB per core
Top-tier single-core, image processing, and text processing benchmark performance
Advanced vector and matrix extension support including AVX-512 and AMX
No local storage or GPU, utilizing 22 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Suited for software compilation, static web serving, and CPU-based inference
